claims.claim: Multi-Head Attention applies learned projections so each head attends from a different representation subspace.
Each head can be written as head_i = Attention(QW_i^Q, KW_i^K, VW_i^V).
The outputs of multiple heads are concatenated and then projected with W^O.
In the original model, d_k and d_v are set to d_model divided by the number of heads.
MQA/GQA, FlashAttention, KV Cache, and MoE belong to different engineering layers and should not be described as the same mechanism.
claims.note: Modern engineering boundary is an explanatory extension; it should be presented as a system-layer distinction, not as an original 2017 paper claim.
